name: Pauoa Flats Trail
tagline: A misty forest trail to a rare flat overlook above Nu'uanu Valley — the trailhead most people walk right past.
parking: Free roadside parking along Tantalus Drive (Round Top Drive) near the trailhead
bestTime: Early morning when the mist hangs in the valley — bring a rain layer
talkStory: Pauoa Flats is one of the only level clearings in the Ko'olau Range — ancient Hawaiians used it as a resting point on the footpath connecting Honolulu to the windward side. The native 'ohi'a lehua and koa forest here is some of the most accessible old-growth near any city in the state. In Hawaiian mythology, the lehua blossom embodies a lover separated from the tree by Pele — picking the flowers brings rain. Connects to the Tantalus network for loops.
